html.note: First full visual QA after logo change from favicon (ID 6) to full IGNITE SVG (ID 214)

compositionGate.summary: Overall composition matches Figma at all breakpoints. Dark header bar with rounded corners, logo on left spanning full height, utility row and nav items on right, mobile SEP button above nav bar.

observations: Dark header bar with charcoal background and 25px border-radius matches Figma exactly
IGNITE logo (bee icon + wordmark) is clearly visible in white on left side of header, spanning the full height of the bar
Logo proportions appear correct — constrained to max-height 58px matching Figma's 57.685px logo height
Utility row on upper-right: search field with underline + vertical separator + SEP link with external arrow icon
Nav items row on lower-right: ABOUT, HEALTH, FINANCIAL WELLBEING, ADVOCACY, SOCIAL, STUDENT VOICES — all uppercase General Sans Medium
Visual gap between utility row and nav items row appears consistent with Figma's 24px gap

measurements.implementationValue: Visible as uppercase medium-weight sans-serif text in screenshots, matching Figma reference

observations: Header layout is consistent with 1440px but in a narrower viewport
Logo remains clearly visible on left, utility row and nav items on right
Nav items wrap correctly when needed at narrower widths
All elements maintain proper spacing and alignment
At 768px (md breakpoint), header transitions to mobile layout
SEP button appears above the nav bar as a full-width rounded pill
Logo on left, hamburger menu toggle on right within the nav bar
Logo constrained to smaller mobile size (max-h-[26px])
Layout matches Figma mobile closed state
Mobile closed state matches Figma: SEP button above, logo + hamburger in nav bar
Logo visible as small IGNITE wordmark with bee icon, constrained to ~26px height
Hamburger icon on the right side of nav bar
SEP button has charcoal background, white text, rounded-full corners, external arrow icon

crossBrowser.summary: Rendering is visually consistent across all three browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) at all four breakpoints. No browser-specific rendering differences observed in the screenshots.

logoSpanningAnalysis.figmaDesign: In Figma, the logo is a direct sibling of the right column (utility row + nav items) within a single flex container, making it visually span both rows.
logoSpanningAnalysis.implementation: In the implementation, the logo is inside a flex row with only the utility row. The nav items are in a separate <nav> element below. However, the logo uses self-stretch to fill the height of the utility row, and the overall visual result closely matches Figma because both rows are still within the same rounded header-main container.
logoSpanningAnalysis.visualResult: The visual output at all breakpoints closely matches the Figma design. The logo appears to span the full height of the header bar. The structural difference (nav items in separate element) does not produce a visible discrepancy.
logoSpanningAnalysis.verdict: PASS — visually equivalent to Figma design

notes.description: The logo SVG uses fill='currentColor' which inherits text color from the parent. There is no explicit text-white class on the logo link element, but the color appears to be inherited correctly from a parent or global style, resulting in white logo text on the dark background.

The nav items row is structurally separate from the logo+utility flex row (it lives inside a <nav> element that is a sibling, not inside the same flex container). This differs from the Figma structure where both rows are in a single right-column flex-col. However, the visual result is equivalent because both elements are still within the same rounded header-main container with shared padding.

The SEP link and mobile CTA button use font-sans (General Sans) rather than Roboto Medium as specified in Figma. Visually the difference is very subtle at 16px uppercase medium weight. This was likely an intentional simplification during development.
